value.title: Is Nuclear Power Bad for the Environment? - Friends of the Earth
value.content: ## Search. Search Friends of the Earth. When you factor in **uranium mining**, **radioactive waste**, **accident risks**, **water use**, **thermal pollution**, **proliferation risks**, and the **centuries-long burden passed to future generations**, nuclear energy becomes far from the “clean” solution it’s marketed to be. Once contaminated, ecosystems can take decades — sometimes centuries — to recover. These mining and milling impacts rarely make it into pro-nuclear marketing — but they are a core part of nuclear’s environmental cost. #### Nuclear accidents can cause:. ## Lifecycle Emissions: Nuclear Isn’t Emissions-Free. No political system today can guarantee safe management for the next 100,000 years. ## Why Nuclear is Not a Climate Solution. That’s not the sustainable, just, or safe path to a livable climate. Real climate solutions don’t gamble with the environment or human health. From uranium mining to radioactive waste, from water contamination to catastrophic risk, nuclear power creates environmental harms that last for generations. ### Donate Today.
